revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:船越神社 (志摩市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image=Funakoshi-jinja(Shima-shi) 01.JPG | name=Funakoshi Shrine | location=861 Funakoshi, Daio-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture | coordinates={{Coord|34|16|12|N|136|51|55|E}} | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} | Rank=Former Village Shrine | founded=Unknown | honden={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | festival=October 18 }}
'''Funakoshi Shrine''' (船越神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|en|Shima, Mie|qid=Q338009|lt=Shima City|1=Shima, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. Its former shrine Rank under the Modern Shrine Ranking System was "Village Shrine."
== Deities Enshrined == The principal deity is {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, alongside the following eight deities: * {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2983724}} (formerly enshrined at Niejima Shrine) * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ojin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (formerly enshrined at Hachiman Shrine) * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} (formerly enshrined at Aijin Shrine) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi no Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (formerly enshrined at Yama Shrine) * Four unidentified deities (including a fire deity from the former Akiba Shrine and the "Horse Hoof Stone" believed to be linked to the deity of the former Nanahonmatsu Shrine)<ref name="daiou">"Daio Town History"</ref>
The exact dates of enshrinement for these deities are unknown.
== History == Funakoshi, the location of the shrine, historically supplied fresh fish as offerings to {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. This practice continued through the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} and {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, with records referring to the area as "Higashi Funakoshi Mikuriya," an estate of {{ill|Ise Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Inner Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The village shrine was originally a {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11590310}}<ref name="jinjasi">"Mie Prefecture Shrine Register"</ref>.
Due to its role as an offering site, the {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2983724}} was also enshrined nearby at Niejima Shrine<ref name="daiou"/>. Territorial disputes occurred with the neighboring Namikiri district over fishing rights, which were eventually settled in 1880.
During the Edo period, Funakoshi Shrine came under the control of the {{ill|Kuki clan|ja|九鬼氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuki clan|1=Kuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11370490}}. The shrine was later renamed "Tsushima Shrine" in 1871 but reverted to "Funakoshi Shrine" in 1876 to align with historical records<ref name="daiou"/><ref name="jinjasi"/>.
Several smaller shrines were merged into Funakoshi Shrine in 1907, including Hachiman Shrine, Aijin Shrine, Yama Shrine, Akiba Shrine, Nanahonmatsu Shrine, Funakoshi Shrine, and Niejima Shrine.
In 1958, a {{ill|Cessna aircraft|ja|セスナ機|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cessna aircraft|1=Cessna aircraft|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17994981}} crashed on the shrine grounds, resulting in the deaths of four crew members<ref>{{Cite book |language=ja |editor=Edited by Nikkai Associates |title=Japanese Disaster History Encyclopedia 1868-2009 |publisher=Nikkai Associates |year=2010 |page=127 |isbn=9784816922749}}</ref>.
== Shrine Buildings == The shrine's buildings underwent renovations in 1879, 1907, 1939, and 1961. The current honden (main hall) is made of reinforced concrete in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} style, featuring five {{ill|katsuogi|ja|鰹木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/katsuogi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木|1=chigi and katsuogi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q87220234}} and inward-slanting {{ill|Chigi|en|Chigi|qid=Q362820|lt=chigi|1=Chigi}}<ref name="daiou"/>.
== Shrine Crest == The shrine crest ({{ill|Mon (emblem)|en|Mon (emblem)|qid=Q600964|lt=kamon|1=Mon (emblem)}}) is the "Five Melons with Karahana" motif.
== Grounds == The shrine grounds cover 6,800 m²<ref name="jinjasi"/>.
; Horse Hoof Stone A stone believed to be associated with a deity's horse hoof, relocated during the 1907 shrine mergers. It is 1.5m tall with two 10cm depressions, reflecting ancient {{ill|megalithic worship|ja|巨石信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/megalithic worship|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="daiou"/>.
; Guardian Lion-Dogs Two pairs of {{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|qid=Q20155846|lt=komainu|1=Komainu}} stand along the approach to the shrine.
== Treasures == * A 79cm-long {{ill|Tachi|en|Tachi|qid=Q120676|lt=tachi (sword)|1=Tachi}} forged during the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|qid=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era|1=Bunka}} in {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|qid=Q514506|lt=Owari Province|1=Owari Province}}<ref name="daiou"/>.
== Access == * Nearest Station: {{ill|Ugata Station|en|Ugata Station|qid=Q1447324|lt=Ugata Station|1=Ugata Station}} on the {{ill|Shima Line|en|Shima Line|qid=Q5357604|lt=Kintetsu Shima Line|1=Shima Line}}, approximately 11 km away. * Bus Stop: About 5 minutes' walk from Funakoshi-hama Bus Stop.
== References == * Daio Town History Editorial Committee, "Daio Town History," 1994. * Mie Prefecture Shrine Register, 1993. * Amino Yoshihiko, Ishii Susumu, Inagaki Yasuhiko, Nagahara Keiji, "Lectures on Japanese Manorial History Vol.6," Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1993. ISBN 4-642-02696-7
== Related Pages == * {{ill|Shinsen|en|Shinsen|qid=Q11591045|lt=Shinsen|1=Shinsen}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat-inline|Funakoshi-jinja (Shima, Mie)}}
{{translated page|ja|船越神社 (志摩市)|version=98302325|comment=Imported full ja history}}
